title: NELFUND Student Loan Portal — How to Apply (Step-by-Step)
summary: A complete guide on how to apply for the NELFUND interest-free student loan in Nigeria — eligibility, documents required, and what happens after approval.
body: Step 1: Visit nelfund.gov.ng and click 'Apply Now'. Step 2: Register with your NIN (National Identification Number) and BVN. Step 3: Upload required documents — admission letter, school ID, guarantor form. Step 4: Submit your application and await review. Step 5: Once approved, funds are disbursed directly to your institution for tuition. Living allowance is sent to your bank account monthly. Repayment begins 2 years after graduation or when you secure employment.

title: Who is Eligible for the NELFUND Student Loan?
summary: NELFUND eligibility criteria — who can apply, what institutions qualify, and what disqualifies an applicant.
body: You are eligible if: (1) You are a Nigerian citizen. (2) You are enrolled in a federal or state tertiary institution (university, polytechnic or college of education). (3) You do not have a history of default on any government loan. (4) You can provide a guarantor — a civil servant on Grade Level 12 or above, or a lawyer, doctor or other professional. Private institution students are currently not covered but this may change in future updates.

title: NELFUND Loan Repayment — What You Need to Know
summary: How and when you pay back the NELFUND student loan — repayment timeline, deductions and what happens if you do not repay.
body: Repayment begins 2 years after graduation OR when you secure employment — whichever comes first. Repayments are deducted directly from your salary at 10% of your monthly income through the PAYE (Pay As You Earn) system. The loan is interest-free, meaning you only pay back exactly what you borrowed. Defaulting on the loan may affect your BVN, NIN and credit history. Civil servants and private sector employees are tracked through FIRS and the National Social Insurance Trust Fund.
